overview.title: AXTP Protocol
overview.summary: AXTP is a transport-independent device communication protocol for CONTROL, RPC and STREAM payloads across Standard Framed transports, plus a formal WebSocket Unframed JSON RPC profile. Phase 1 requires the STREAM data plane for audio/video media flow profiles.
goals: Provide one unified protocol model for control, request/response RPC and audio/video stream transfer.
Make Standard Frame the AXTP v1 Core binary path for USB HID High Speed and TCP.
Support WebSocket Unframed JSON as the formal RPC-only integration path.
Keep full dynamic capability modeling optional outside AXTP v1 Core.
nonGoals: Full dynamic UI capability modeling is not required in v1.
Compact/HID-64/BLE/UART low-bandwidth framing is not required by AXTP v1 Core.
STREAM is not carried over WebSocket Unframed JSON.
Header profile negotiation is not performed dynamically in v1.

layers.name: Transport Profile
layers.description: Defines how AXTP runs over USB HID High Speed, TCP, or WebSocket JSON.

layers.name: Frame Profile
layers.description: Standard Frame is the v1 Core binary frame profile; unframed JSON skips the Frame layer.

layers.name: Payload Layer
layers.description: Carries CONTROL, RPC or STREAM payloads.

layers.name: RPC Session
layers.description: Handles Hello, Identify, requests, responses, events and errors.

layers.name: Stream Session
layers.description: Handles Phase 1 audio/video stream transfer and later OTA, file, log and sensor chunk transfer profiles.

lifecycle.description: Open an AXTP logical session and declare runtime limits.
Accept the session and return final runtime parameters.
Announce RPC session rules, AXTP version and authentication requirements.
Submit client identity, randomSeed uint32 and optional authentication data.
Confirm that the RPC session is ready.
Use the generated protocol registry to select adopted business methods for the current product.

optionalLifecycleExtensions.description: Reserved for transports that need an explicit client acknowledgement after ACCEPT; not required by AXTP v1 Core.

quickStart.title: Minimal Client Startup
steps: Connect using AXTP-USB-HID, AXTP-TCP, or AXTP-WS-JSON.
For Standard Framed transports, send CONTROL OPEN and wait for CONTROL ACCEPT.
For WebSocket Unframed JSON, skip CONTROL and wait for Hello after the WebSocket opens.
Load generated protocol registry for the current product build.

quickStart.title: WebSocket Unframed JSON Startup
steps: Wait for Hello from the Logical Server.
Send Identify using the JSON sid/op/d envelope.
Load generated protocol registry for the current product build.
Start JSON RPC requests and receive JSON events.

quickStart.title: Cloud Reverse Connection
steps: Device opens the WebSocket connection to the cloud endpoint.
No CONTROL OPEN or Standard Frame is used in this profile.
Device remains the Logical Server and sends Hello after the WebSocket is established.
Cloud identifies as the Logical Client and then issues JSON RPC requests.

wire.integerEncoding: unsigned and signed multi-byte integers use Big-Endian / network byte order
wire.scope: Standard Framed Binary, CONTROL, RPC JSON_BINARY, TLV, STREAM header, and CRC footer

transports.name: AXTP-USB-HID
transports.notes: USB HID High Speed or large-report HID transport using Standard Frame.

transports.name: AXTP-TCP
transports.notes: TCP byte stream transport using Standard Frame magic and length parsing.

transports.name: AXTP-WS-JSON
transports.notes: Formal RPC-only WebSocket JSON profile using the sid/op/d envelope.

transports.name: AXTP-WS-CLOUD-REVERSE
transports.notes: Device initiates the WebSocket connection but remains the Logical Server.

payloadTypes.name: RPC
payloadTypes.description: RPC payload starts with rpcEncoding; JSON_BINARY then carries the fixed binary envelope.

rules: Standard Framed transports require OPEN and ACCEPT before RPC or STREAM payloads.
Phase 1 runtimes must implement HEARTBEAT/HEARTBEAT_ACK and CLOSE/CLOSE_ACK for framed links.
WebSocket Unframed JSON does not use CONTROL payloads.
READY is not required by v1 Core and must not be assumed by peers.
ACK/NACK opcodes and fields are reserved for future reliable-transfer profiles and are not Phase 1 requirements.
Dynamic header negotiation is not performed in v1.
STREAM uses the unified 16-byte header in Standard Framed transports.
WebSocket Unframed JSON must not carry STREAM data.
The historical 8-byte stream header is not part of AXTP v1 Core.
Phase 1 Standard Framed runtimes must implement STREAM data packets for adopted audio/video media stream profiles.
Stable methodId, eventId and error code values must not be reused.
Legacy command mappings may be preserved as metadata under methods[].legacy.
Low-bandwidth Compact transports are documented as a degradation path outside AXTP v1 Core.

fields.description: Pairing code usage scenario; candidate values include initial_enrollment (first-time enrollment of a new device, default), re_enrollment (re-enrollment such as workspace migration), and service_repair (service/repair pairing, may have a different TTL or permission). Unsupported purpose returns NOT_SUPPORTED.

schemas.name: DevicePairingCodeInfo
schemas.description: Pairing code snapshot returned by device.getPairingCode. The code field carries a displayable or inputtable code; expiresAt is the authoritative absolute expiry while expiresInSeconds is retained for legacy device-sdk compatibility.

fields.name: code
fields.description: Displayable pairing code, 6-8 uppercase alphanumeric characters excluding confusable characters (0/O, 1/I/L); MUST match the regex ^[A-HJ-NP-Z2-9]{6,8}$.

fields.name: expiresAt
fields.description: Absolute expiry time as an RFC 3339 timestamp. Authoritative when present. Legacy GetBindCode returned a Unix timestamp integer here; the adapter MUST convert integer to RFC 3339 string.

fields.name: expiresInSeconds
fields.description: Relative expiry in seconds, retained for legacy device-sdk compatibility (observed value 1800). MUST be greater than 0 when present.

fields.name: state
fields.description: Pairing code lifecycle state; candidate values include available (default), expired (TTL reached), used (consumed by the cloud), and disabled (revoked by the server).

schemas.name: DeviceEnrollmentInfo

fields.name: state
fields.description: Current enrollment state; candidate values include unmanaged, pairing_available, pending, enrolled, failed, and unenrolling. Valid transitions follow the enrollment state machine; invalid transitions return INVALID_STATE.

fields.name: deviceId
fields.description: Server-assigned device identifier, stable across sessions.

fields.name: workspaceId
fields.description: Enrolled workspace identifier. Populated only when state is enrolled or unenrolling. Privacy-sensitive; exposure policy to be confirmed. [REVIEW-ADOPTED-SCOPED] 待确认后走 amend

fields.name: endpoint
fields.description: Post-enrollment backend endpoint. Populated only when state is enrolled or unenrolling and subject to includeEndpoint.

fields.name: enrolledAt
fields.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of when the state became enrolled. Present only when state is enrolled or unenrolling. [REVIEW-ADOPTED-SCOPED]

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the most recent state update.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable detail, populated when state is failed or pending, optional when unenrolling.

schemas.name: DeviceEnrollmentEndpointSummary

fields.name: type
fields.description: Endpoint type; candidate values include room, device, and asset. Operations such as room.setName only allow type=room.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: Endpoint display name, non-empty and trimmed. Max length aligned with device.info product.displayName (128) as a scoped default. [REVIEW-ADOPTED-SCOPED] 待产品确认后走 amend

fields.name: profileId
fields.description: Room profile or business profile identifier. MUST be present when type is room; other types allow omission.

fields.name: workspaceId
fields.description: Endpoint workspace identifier. Takes precedence over the parent DeviceEnrollmentInfo.workspaceId when both are present. [REVIEW-ADOPTED-SCOPED]

schemas.name: DeviceSetEnrollmentStateParams
schemas.description: Request for device.setEnrollmentState. Validation rules follow the enrollment state machine. For desiredState enrolled, only reachable from pending, endpoint and reason required, and endpoint.profileId required when endpoint.type is room. For desiredState unmanaged, reachable from enrolled/failed/pairing_available, reason recommended. For desiredState failed, only reachable from pending, message required. For desiredState pending, reachable from failed (retry) or pairing_available (code submitted). Transitions must follow the state machine; violations return INVALID_STATE.

fields.name: desiredState
fields.description: Target state; candidate values include enrolled, unmanaged, failed, and pending.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Change reason; candidate values include pairing_code_used, server_claimed, user_unenrolled, admin_reset, and unknown (default).

fields.name: endpoint
fields.description: Endpoint summary associated with a successful enrollment. Required when desiredState is enrolled.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Failure, unbind, or repair detail. Required when desiredState is failed.

schemas.name: DeviceSetEnrollmentStateResult
schemas.description: Result of device.setEnrollmentState; carries the post-operation enrollment snapshot.

fields.name: disconnectExpected
fields.description: Whether the unbind or reset is expected to cause a connection change. true only when desiredState is unmanaged and the device needs to close the management session; false for all other transitions.

schemas.name: DeviceEnrollmentStateChangedEvent
schemas.description: Event payload emitted when the enrollment state changes.

fields.name: previousState
fields.description: State enum value (not a full object) before the change; candidate values are the same DeviceEnrollmentInfo.state set.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Change reason; candidate values include pairing_code_used, server_claimed, user_unenrolled, admin_reset, and unknown (default).

fields.name: triggerMethod
fields.description: Operation type that triggered the change; candidate values include setEnrollmentState (explicit call), getPairingCode (indirect, for code-expiry fallback), and server_sync (device-internal such as code-expiry rollback or server-side revoke). triggerId MUST be omitted when triggerMethod is server_sync. [REVIEW-ADOPTED-SCOPED]

fields.name: triggerId
fields.description: RPC request id of the triggering operation. Omitted when triggerMethod is server_sync. [REVIEW-ADOPTED-SCOPED]

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Change reason; candidate values include user_request (triggered by setConfig), restore_default (triggered by resetConfig), device_policy (triggered by device-internal policy), and unknown (default when the reason is not reported).

schemas.name: LauncherConfig
schemas.description: Configuration fragment for target launcher. Referenced by SoftwareConfig.config, SoftwareSetConfigParams.config, and SoftwareConfigChangedEvent.config when target is launcher.

fields.name: displayName
fields.description: User-visible device display name. Overrides the device factory name returned read-only by device.info product.displayName. MUST be non-empty; an empty string or value exceeding the max length returns INVALID_ARGUMENT. Omitted means keep current value on set, or the device factory name on get/reset.

fields.name: appearance
fields.description: Appearance configuration for the launcher panel.

schemas.name: LauncherAppearance
schemas.description: Appearance configuration for the launcher panel, nested under LauncherConfig.appearance.

fields.name: panelLayout
fields.description: Panel layout mode; candidate values are focus (focus mode) and sidebar (sidebar mode, default).

fields.name: autoHidePanel
fields.description: Whether to auto-hide the panel. Default is false.

fields.name: autoHideDelay
fields.description: Auto-hide delay in seconds; MUST be greater than 0. Only effective when autoHidePanel is true. The value is preserved and persisted even when autoHidePanel is false and takes effect again once autoHidePanel switches back to true.

schemas.name: SoftwareConfigCapability
schemas.description: Capability descriptor for software.config.

fields.name: supportedTargets
fields.description: Supported software object target strings; candidate values include launcher, signagePlayer, and agent.

fields.name: supportsReset
fields.description: Whether restoring defaults via resetConfig is supported.

fields.name: resetMayRestartSoftware
fields.description: Whether reset may restart the software object (not the device). System-level factory reset is handled by system.restoreFactorySettings.

schemas.name: SoftwareGetUpdatePolicyParams
schemas.description: Request for software.getUpdatePolicy; selects the software object to read by target.

fields.name: target
fields.description: Software object to read the update policy of. Currently defined value is launcher; other values such as signagePlayer and agent are reserved for future adoption and their policy fields are not defined in this version.

schemas.name: SoftwareUpdatePolicy
schemas.description: Update policy snapshot of a software object. For the launcher target, policy carries the LauncherUpdatePolicy structure (updateMode, schedule, channel, conditions). When target is launcher, schedule and conditions may be null to explicitly clear the time window or prerequisites; null is distinct from omitted.

fields.name: target
fields.description: Software object this update policy belongs to.

fields.name: policy
fields.description: Target-specific update policy fragment. For the launcher target this is the LauncherUpdatePolicy structure (updateMode plus schedule, channel, conditions); see the LauncherUpdatePolicy, UpdateSchedule, and UpdateConditions schemas.

schemas.name: SoftwareSetUpdatePolicyParams
schemas.description: Request for software.setUpdatePolicy; applies a partial update to the target software object. Fields absent from policy are left unchanged; policy itself must be a valid object and does not accept null. Within the launcher policy fragment, schedule null explicitly clears the time window and conditions null explicitly clears all prerequisites; omitted means keep current value.

fields.name: target
fields.description: Software object to configure the update policy of.

fields.name: policy
fields.description: Target-specific update policy fragment to apply (partial update). For the launcher target this is the LauncherUpdatePolicy structure; see SoftwareUpdatePolicy.policy. Within the fragment, schedule null or conditions null explicitly clears those sub-objects while omitted keeps them unchanged.

schemas.name: SoftwareResetUpdatePolicyParams
schemas.description: Request for software.resetUpdatePolicy; restores the specified software object update policy to its current-version defaults.

fields.name: target
fields.description: Software object to reset the update policy of.

schemas.name: SoftwareUpdatePolicyChangedEvent
schemas.description: Event payload emitted when a software object update policy changes.

fields.name: target
fields.description: Software object whose update policy changed.

fields.name: policy
fields.description: Full post-change update policy fragment (not a patch). For the launcher target this is the LauncherUpdatePolicy structure; see SoftwareUpdatePolicy.policy.

fields.name: changedFields
fields.description: Optional changed field paths using dot notation for nested levels, e.g. schedule.start. Omitted when the device cannot compute the diff.

fields.name: reason
fields.description: Change reason; candidate values include user_request (triggered by setUpdatePolicy), restore_default (triggered by resetUpdatePolicy), device_policy (triggered by device-internal policy), and unknown (default when the reason is not reported).

schemas.name: LauncherUpdatePolicy
schemas.description: Update policy fragment for target launcher. Referenced by SoftwareUpdatePolicy.policy, SoftwareSetUpdatePolicyParams.policy, and SoftwareUpdatePolicyChangedEvent.policy when target is launcher. schedule null means no update time restriction (any time) and conditions null means no prerequisites; both are valid persisted values and the reset default sets both to null. When updateMode is manual or notify, schedule may still be set and persisted but the device does not automatically perform updates in the window; the schedule value is preserved across updateMode changes.

fields.name: updateMode
fields.description: Update behavior mode; candidate values are auto (fully automatic download and install), manual (manual trigger only), and notify (notify only when an update is available).

fields.name: schedule
fields.description: Automatic update time window. Only effective when updateMode is auto. null means no time restriction (any time of day). In setUpdatePolicy partial update, null explicitly clears the window while omitted keeps the current value. See UpdateSchedule.

fields.name: channel
fields.description: Update channel; candidate values are release (stable), beta (test), and alpha (development).

fields.name: conditions
fields.description: Automatic update prerequisites. null means no prerequisites (no restriction). In setUpdatePolicy partial update, null explicitly clears all conditions while omitted keeps the current value. See UpdateConditions.

schemas.name: UpdateSchedule
schemas.description: Automatic update time window, nested under LauncherUpdatePolicy.schedule.

fields.name: start
fields.description: Window start time in local time, matching the regex ^([01]\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\d$ (HH:mm). A value that does not match returns INVALID_ARGUMENT.

fields.name: end
fields.description: Window end time in local time, matching the regex ^([01]\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\d$ (HH:mm). A value that does not match returns INVALID_ARGUMENT. When end is earlier than start it denotes a cross-midnight window (from start on the current day to end on the next day).

fields.name: timezone
fields.description: IANA timezone ID. Omitted means the device local timezone.

schemas.name: UpdateConditions
schemas.description: Automatic update prerequisites, nested under LauncherUpdatePolicy.conditions.

fields.name: requireIdle
fields.description: Whether the device must be idle before performing an update.

fields.name: requireWifi
fields.description: Whether updates may only be downloaded over a Wi-Fi network.

schemas.name: SoftwareUpdatePolicyCapability
schemas.description: Capability descriptor for software.updatePolicy.

fields.name: supportedTargets
fields.description: Supported software object target strings; candidate values include launcher, signagePlayer, and agent.

fields.name: supportedChannels
fields.description: Optional supported update channel strings; candidate values include release, beta, and alpha.

fields.name: supportsSchedule
fields.description: Whether configuring an update time window via schedule is supported.

fields.name: supportsReset
fields.description: Whether restoring defaults via resetUpdatePolicy is supported.

schemas.name: VideoGetStreamCapabilitiesParams
schemas.description: Selector for video stream capability.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Optional video source identifier; omit to query all visible sources.

fields.name: includeRuntimeState
fields.description: Whether to include current source runtime state.

schemas.name: VideoStreamCapabilities
schemas.description: Device-level video.stream capability summary.

fields.name: openModes
fields.description: Supported open modes, such as producer_open and receiver_pull.

fields.name: peerRoles
fields.description: Peer roles, such as receiver and transmitter.

fields.name: supportsSyncGroup
fields.description: Whether video streams can share a synchronization group with audio streams.

fields.name: flowControlManagedByRuntime
fields.description: Whether normal applications can rely on runtime-managed STREAM flow control.

schemas.name: VideoStreamSource
schemas.description: One real-time video stream source.

fields.name: state
fields.description: Runtime source state, such as available, receiving, stopped, or unavailable.

schemas.name: VideoOpenStreamParams
schemas.description: Request to open a real-time video stream.

fields.name: peerRole
fields.description: Requested peer media role; values include receiver and transmitter.

fields.name: codec
fields.description: Requested video codec, such as h264, h265, mjpeg, or raw.

fields.name: cursorUnit
fields.description: STREAM cursor unit, such as timestampUs or frameIndex.

fields.name: maxDataSize
fields.description: Preferred maximum STREAM payload data size.

schemas.name: VideoOpenStreamResult
schemas.description: Result of opening a real-time video stream.

fields.name: state
fields.description: Initial state, normally opening or streaming.

fields.name: maxDataSize
fields.description: Negotiated maximum STREAM payload data size.

schemas.name: VideoCloseStreamParams
schemas.description: Request to close a video stream.

schemas.name: VideoCloseStreamResult
schemas.description: Result of closing a video stream.

fields.name: state
fields.description: Close state, such as closing, closed, or failed.

fields.name: alreadyClosed
fields.description: Whether the stream was already terminal before this request.

schemas.name: VideoGetStreamStateParams
schemas.description: Selector for a video stream state query.

schemas.name: VideoStreamState
schemas.description: Runtime state of one video stream.

fields.name: state
fields.description: Stream state, such as opening, streaming, closing, closed, or failed.

fields.name: keyFrameRequested
fields.description: Whether a key frame has been requested and is pending.

fields.name: failureReason
fields.description: Failure reason when state is failed.

schemas.name: VideoGetStreamSourceStateParams
schemas.description: Selector for video source runtime state.

schemas.name: VideoStreamSourceState
schemas.description: Runtime state of a video stream source.

fields.name: state
fields.description: Source state, such as unavailable, available, receiving, stopped, or failed.

fields.name: available
fields.description: Whether the source is available for openStream.

fields.name: activeStreamId
fields.description: Active downstream stream id, if any.

schemas.name: VideoRequestKeyFrameParams
schemas.description: Request a key frame for an active video stream.

schemas.name: VideoRequestKeyFrameResult
schemas.description: Result of a key frame request.

schemas.name: VideoStreamStateChangedEvent
schemas.description: Event payload for video stream state changes.

schemas.name: VideoStreamSourceStateChangedEvent
schemas.description: Event payload for video stream source state changes.

fields.name: activeStreamId
fields.description: Active downstream stream id, if any.

schemas.name: VideoStreamStatsReportedEvent
schemas.description: Event payload for video stream statistics reports.

schemas.name: VideoStreamStats
schemas.description: Bounded runtime statistics for a video stream.

fields.name: bytes
fields.description: Number of STREAM payload bytes observed.

methods.name: audio.getAlgorithmCapabilities
methods.description: Return supported audio algorithm objects, fields, defaults, ranges, units, and update policy.

methods.name: audio.getAlgorithmConfig
methods.description: Return the current effective configuration for supported audio algorithm objects.

methods.name: audio.setAlgorithmConfig
methods.description: Partially update one or more audio algorithm configuration objects atomically.

methods.name: audio.resetAlgorithmConfig
methods.description: Reset all, selected, or selected-field audio algorithm configuration to declared default values.

methods.name: audio.getStreamCapabilities
methods.description: Return real-time audio stream sources, codecs, stream profiles, and open-mode support.

methods.name: audio.openStream
methods.description: Open a real-time audio STREAM and return the negotiated streamId and media metadata.

methods.name: audio.closeStream
methods.description: Close a previously opened audio STREAM.

methods.name: audio.getStreamState
methods.description: Return runtime state for an opened audio stream.

methods.name: audio.getStreamSourceState
methods.description: Return availability and receiving state for an audio stream source.

methods.name: cast.getSession
methods.description: Return the current cast receiver phase and active session summary.

methods.name: cast.stopSession
methods.description: Stop the current cast session when one is active.

methods.name: cast.getAirPlayName
methods.description: Return the AirPlay receiver display name and publish state.

methods.name: cast.setAirPlayName
methods.description: Set the AirPlay receiver display name and request service rediscovery.

methods.name: cast.getAudio
methods.description: Return local cast audio playback and mute state.

methods.name: cast.setAudio
methods.description: Enable or disable local playback of received cast audio.

methods.name: cast.setMuted
methods.description: Mute or unmute local cast audio output.

methods.name: cast.getPinCodeConfig
methods.description: Return cast PIN protection state, visibility, and optional secret material.

methods.name: cast.setPinCodeConfig
methods.description: Enable or disable cast PIN protection and update display policy.

methods.name: cast.setPinCode
methods.description: Set the current cast PIN value according to receiver policy.

methods.name: cast.getWindowState
methods.description: Return cast window visibility, mode, and bounds summary.

methods.name: cast.setWindowState
methods.description: Set cast window mode, fullscreen state, or always-on-top state.

methods.name: cast.getBackendStatus
methods.description: Return cast backend process, discoverability, and last-error state.

methods.name: cast.restartBackend
methods.description: Restart the cast backend without restarting the receiver application or device.

methods.name: cast.getFlowControlState
methods.description: Return local cast render fps, queue policy, and low-frequency diagnostics.

methods.name: cast.setRenderFps
methods.description: Set the receiver-local target render fps for cast media.

methods.name: cast.setFlowPolicy
methods.description: Set receiver-local cast queue, late-frame, drop, and overlay policy.

methods.name: cast.getStatus
methods.description: Return a current cast receiver snapshot for UI reload, reconnect, or event-loss recovery.

methods.name: device.getInfo
methods.description: Return the current endpoint main device identity, product, hardware, OS, software, AXTP runtime, and optional capability summary.

methods.name: device.getPairingCode
methods.description: Return a pairing code for on-site or backend-claimed device enrollment. With refresh=false (default) the current valid code is returned; with refresh=true the server generates a new code and invalidates the previous one.

methods.name: device.getEnrollmentState
methods.description: Query whether the device is enrolled and return the associated backend object summary when enrolled.

methods.name: device.setEnrollmentState
methods.description: Set the enrollment state (bind success, unbind, clear failure, or sync server claim). State transitions must follow the enrollment state machine; an invalid transition returns INVALID_STATE. Emitted after the state actually changes by the device.enrollmentStateChanged event.

methods.name: firmware.getUpdateCapabilities
methods.description: Return P0 firmware update capability and upload constraints.

methods.name: firmware.beginUpdate
methods.description: Create a firmware update session, accept the manifest, and bind file IDs to STREAM streamIds.

methods.name: firmware.finishUpdate
methods.description: Tell the device that upload is complete and hand off verification, install, and reboot to the device.

methods.name: firmware.getUpdateState
methods.description: Return current firmware update state for UI refresh, reconnect, or event-loss recovery.

methods.name: network.getInterfaces
methods.description: Return visible network interfaces and default interface identifiers.

methods.name: network.getInterfaceInfo
methods.description: Return detailed information for one network interface.

methods.name: network.getIpConfig
methods.description: Return IP configuration for a network interface.

methods.name: network.setIpConfig
methods.description: Set DHCP/static/disabled IP configuration for a network interface.

methods.name: network.getWifiCapabilities
methods.description: Return Wi-Fi station capability, including security types, bands, scanning, and credential import modes.

methods.name: network.getWifiConfig
methods.description: Return saved Wi-Fi profile summaries without plaintext credentials.

methods.name: network.setWifiConfig
methods.description: Create or update a Wi-Fi station profile.

methods.name: network.scanWifi
methods.description: Scan visible Wi-Fi access points.

methods.name: network.connectWifi
methods.description: Connect to a saved Wi-Fi profile or an inline profile.

methods.name: network.disconnectWifi
methods.description: Disconnect the current Wi-Fi station connection.

methods.name: network.getWifiState
methods.description: Return current Wi-Fi station association, authentication, and connection state.

methods.name: network.getApCapabilities
methods.description: Return Wi-Fi AP capability, including supported bands, security types, channel ranges, and credential export policy.

methods.name: network.getApConfig
methods.description: Return Wi-Fi AP configuration without exposing plaintext credentials unless explicitly allowed by policy.

methods.name: network.setApConfig
methods.description: Partially update Wi-Fi AP configuration.

methods.name: network.getApState
methods.description: Return runtime state for the device Wi-Fi AP role.

methods.name: network.startAp
methods.description: Start the Wi-Fi AP role.

methods.name: network.stopAp
methods.description: Stop the Wi-Fi AP role.

methods.name: network.getApClients
methods.description: Return clients currently associated with the Wi-Fi AP.

methods.name: signage.getPlaylistCapabilities
methods.description: Return the signage.playlist capability scope, including supported playlist item types, quantity limits, and feature toggles.

methods.name: signage.getPlaylistConfig
methods.description: Return the current playlist configuration held by the device as the complete playlists array.

methods.name: signage.setPlaylistConfig
methods.description: Fully replace the playlist configuration. The server is the single source of truth; a second full sync removes playlist items absent from the new payload.

methods.name: signage.resetPlaylistConfig
methods.description: Restore the playlist configuration to the factory default state.

methods.name: signage.getPlaylistItemUrl
methods.description: Fetch the latest resource URL for a playlist item by itemId (URL refresh). The device calls this proactively when an item URL is about to expire. Clock items have no URL resource and return NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: software.getConfig
methods.description: Read the current runtime configuration of a software object identified by target.

methods.name: software.setConfig
methods.description: Set a configuration fragment of a software object using partial update semantics; fields absent from the payload are left unchanged.

methods.name: software.resetConfig
methods.description: Restore the runtime configuration of a software object to the current-version defaults for the given target. Only resets the specified software object; system-level factory reset is handled by system.restoreFactorySettings and does not trigger a device reboot.

methods.name: software.getUpdatePolicy
methods.description: Read the current automatic update policy of a software object identified by target.

methods.name: software.setUpdatePolicy
methods.description: Set an update policy fragment of a software object using partial update semantics; fields absent from the payload are left unchanged.

methods.name: software.resetUpdatePolicy
methods.description: Restore the update policy of a software object to the current-version defaults for the given target. Only resets the update policy; it does not trigger a software or device reboot. System-level factory reset is handled by system.restoreFactorySettings.

methods.name: video.getStreamCapabilities
methods.description: Return video stream sources, codecs, profiles, and open-mode support.

methods.name: video.openStream
methods.description: Open a real-time video STREAM and return the negotiated streamId and media metadata.

methods.name: video.closeStream
methods.description: Close a previously opened video STREAM.

methods.name: video.getStreamState
methods.description: Return runtime state for an opened video stream.

methods.name: video.getStreamSourceState
methods.description: Return availability and receiving state for a video stream source.

methods.name: video.requestKeyFrame
methods.description: Request an encoder key frame for an active video stream.

events.name: audio.algorithmConfigChanged
events.description: Emitted when audio algorithm configuration changes after set, reset, profile, restore, factory reset, or device policy changes.

events.name: audio.streamStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when an audio stream enters opening, streaming, closed, or failed state.

events.name: audio.streamSourceStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when an audio stream source availability or receiving state changes.

events.name: audio.streamStatsReported
events.description: Emitted with bounded runtime statistics for an audio stream.

events.name: cast.sessionIncoming
events.description: Emitted when a cast source initiates a receiver session.

events.name: cast.sessionStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when receiver phase, protocol state, or low-frequency session media state changes.

events.name: cast.sessionStarted
events.description: Emitted when a cast session becomes user-visible after first frame or local playback starts.

events.name: cast.sessionStopped
events.description: Emitted when a cast session ends normally or is forced to stop.

events.name: cast.sessionFailed
events.description: Emitted when cast connection, authentication, negotiation, or backend handling fails.

events.name: cast.audioChanged
events.description: Emitted when local cast audio playback or mute state changes.

events.name: cast.pinCodeChanged
events.description: Emitted when cast PIN protection configuration or current PIN state changes.

events.name: cast.pinCodeRequired
events.description: Emitted when a cast source is waiting for PIN authentication.

events.name: cast.pinCodeAuthFailed
events.description: Emitted when PIN authentication fails, times out, or is cancelled.

events.name: cast.windowChanged
events.description: Emitted when cast window visibility, mode, topmost state, or bounds changes.

events.name: cast.backendChanged
events.description: Emitted when cast backend ready, restart, exit, failed, or discoverable state changes.

events.name: cast.flowControlChanged
events.description: Emitted when receiver-local cast flow policy or low-frequency flow statistics change.

events.name: device.enrollmentStateChanged
events.description: Emitted after device.setEnrollmentState actually changes the enrollment state, or after a device-internal policy modifies it (e.g. pairing code expiry fallback, server-side revoke).

events.name: firmware.updateProgressReported
events.description: Emitted when firmware receiving, verification, or install progress changes.

events.name: firmware.updateStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when firmware update state changes or fails.

events.name: network.interfaceStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when network interface administrative or link state changes.

events.name: network.ipConfigChanged
events.description: Emitted when IP configuration changes for an interface.

events.name: network.wifiConfigChanged
events.description: Emitted when saved Wi-Fi profile configuration changes.

events.name: network.wifiStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when Wi-Fi station association, authentication, connection, or failure state changes.

events.name: network.wifiScanResultReported
events.description: Emitted for asynchronous Wi-Fi scan results or scan completion.

events.name: network.apConfigChanged
events.description: Emitted when Wi-Fi AP configuration changes.

events.name: network.apStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when Wi-Fi AP runtime state changes.

events.name: network.apClientChanged
events.description: Emitted when a client joins or leaves the Wi-Fi AP.

events.name: signage.playlistConfigChanged
events.description: Emitted after setPlaylistConfig or resetPlaylistConfig successfully changes the playlist configuration.

events.name: software.configChanged
events.description: Emitted after software.setConfig or software.resetConfig successfully changes a software object configuration, or after a device-internal policy modifies it.

events.name: software.updatePolicyChanged
events.description: Emitted after software.setUpdatePolicy or software.resetUpdatePolicy successfully changes a software object update policy, or after a device-internal policy modifies it.

events.name: video.streamStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when a video stream enters opening, streaming, closed, failed, or keyframe-related state.

events.name: video.streamSourceStateChanged
events.description: Emitted when a video stream source availability or receiving state changes.

events.name: video.streamStatsReported
events.description: Emitted with bounded runtime statistics for a video stream.

errors.name: NOT_SUPPORTED
errors.message: Feature is not supported by the current device or mode.

errors.name: INVALID_STATE
errors.message: Operation is not allowed in the current state.

errors.name: FRAME_LENGTH_INVALID
errors.message: Frame payload length or total length is invalid.

errors.name: FRAME_FRAGMENT_MISSING
errors.message: One or more frame fragments are missing.

errors.name: FRAME_TOO_LARGE
errors.message: Frame exceeds the negotiated maximum size.

errors.name: RESERVED_CONTROL_BODY_ENCODING_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Historical control body encoding negotiation error. AXTP v1 implementations must not emit it.

errors.name: CONTROL_OPEN_REQUIRED
errors.message: Session has not completed CONTROL OPEN.

errors.name: RESERVED_CONTROL_PROFILE_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Historical header profile negotiation error. AXTP v1 implementations must not emit it.

errors.name: RPC_BODY_ENCODING_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: RPC body encoding is not supported.

errors.name: RPC_METHOD_NOT_FOUND
errors.message: MethodId or method name is not registered.

errors.name: RPC_METHOD_NOT_SUPPORTED
errors.message: Method exists but is not supported by the current device.

errors.name: RPC_PARAM_OUT_OF_RANGE
errors.message: RPC parameter is out of range.

errors.name: RPC_BATCH_PARTIAL_FAILED
errors.message: One or more RPC batch items failed.

errors.name: DEVICE_MODE_CONFLICT
errors.message: Device mode conflicts with the requested operation.

errors.name: ENROLLMENT_CODE_ALREADY_USED
errors.message: Pairing code has already been used.

errors.name: FW_IMAGE_TYPE_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Firmware image type is not supported.

errors.name: FW_SIZE_MISMATCH
errors.message: Firmware size does not match the declared size.

errors.name: FW_HASH_MISMATCH
errors.message: Firmware hash does not match the declared verification value.

errors.name: FW_STORAGE_NOT_ENOUGH
errors.message: Not enough storage for firmware update.

errors.name: FW_DEVICE_NOT_READY
errors.message: Device is not ready for firmware update.

errors.name: STREAM_OFFSET_INVALID
errors.message: Stream cursor or offset is invalid.

errors.name: MEDIA_SOURCE_NOT_FOUND
errors.message: Requested media source does not exist.

errors.name: MEDIA_SOURCE_UNAVAILABLE
errors.message: Requested media source is currently unavailable.

errors.name: MEDIA_CODEC_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Requested media codec or sample format is unsupported.

errors.name: MEDIA_FRAMERATE_UNSUPPORTED
errors.message: Requested video frame rate is unsupported.

errors.name: MEDIA_STREAM_START_FAILED
errors.message: Device failed to start the requested media stream.

errors.name: MEDIA_STREAM_STOP_FAILED
errors.message: Device failed to stop the media stream.

errors.name: LEGACY_CMD_UNMAPPED
errors.message: Legacy CmdValue is not mapped to an AXTP method.

errors.name: LEGACY_STATUS_UNMAPPED
errors.message: Legacy status is not mapped to an AXTP ErrorCode.

errors.name: LEGACY_CAPABILITY_CONFLICT
errors.message: Legacy capability conflicts with AXTP capability.

capabilities.name: protocol.payload.control
capabilities.description: Device supports CONTROL payload.

capabilities.name: protocol.payload.rpc
capabilities.description: Device supports RPC payload.

capabilities.name: protocol.payload.stream
capabilities.description: Device supports STREAM data-plane payloads.

capabilities.name: protocol.reservedRequestIdWidth

capabilities.name: audio.algorithm
capabilities.description: Device supports runtime audio algorithm capability discovery, configuration, reset, and change notification.

capabilities.name: audio.stream
capabilities.description: Device supports real-time audio STREAM setup, close, state, source state, and stats events.

capabilities.name: cast.session
capabilities.description: Device supports cast receiver session phase, AirPlay name, and session stop control.

capabilities.name: cast.audio
capabilities.description: Device supports local cast audio playback and mute control.

capabilities.name: cast.pinCode
capabilities.description: Device supports cast PIN protection, PIN state, and authentication events.

capabilities.name: cast.window
capabilities.description: Device supports cast window state query and mode control.

capabilities.name: cast.backend
capabilities.description: Device supports cast backend status query and restart control.

capabilities.name: cast.flowControl
capabilities.description: Device supports receiver-local cast render fps, queue, drop, overlay, and diagnostics control.

capabilities.name: cast.status
capabilities.description: Device supports current cast receiver status snapshot query.

capabilities.name: device.info
capabilities.description: Device supports read-only device information discovery.

capabilities.name: device.enrollment

capabilities.name: firmware.update

capabilities.name: network.interface
capabilities.description: Device supports network interface enumeration and state observation.

capabilities.name: network.ip
capabilities.description: Device supports IP configuration query, update, and change notification.

capabilities.name: network.wifi
capabilities.description: Device supports Wi-Fi station profile, scan, connection, and state operations.

capabilities.name: network.ap
capabilities.description: Device supports Wi-Fi AP configuration, runtime state, and client observation.

capabilities.name: signage.playlist
capabilities.description: Device supports digital signage playlist full sync, query, reset, playlist item URL refresh, and playlist config change notification.

capabilities.name: software.config
capabilities.description: Device supports reading, setting, and resetting the runtime configuration of software objects (such as the Launcher) and emitting configuration change notifications.

capabilities.name: software.updatePolicy
capabilities.description: Device supports reading, setting, and resetting the automatic update policy of software objects (such as the Launcher) and emitting update policy change notifications.

capabilities.name: video.stream
capabilities.description: Device supports real-time video STREAM setup, close, state, source state, key frame requests, and stats events.
